content.problemStatement:
Niket is an incredible magician. He has prepared an extraordinary magic trick which he calls as the biggest achievement of his life. He will be performing this magic trick for the first time in the K\text{K}K-Pop World Festival. He is nervous and wants to make sure that everything goes well. His magic trick consists of N\text{N}N acts labeled from 1 to N. Every act except act 1 is directly\text{directly}directly dependent upon the successful execution of exactly one of the previous acts. The act i is said to be dependent upon act j iff -


act i is directly dependent upon the act j (j < i) or
act i is dependent upon some act x which is dependent upon the act j. (j < x < i)
An act can never happen if one or more of its dependencies failed. Note that not happening is not the same as failing\text{not happening is not the same as failing}not happening is not the same as failing. Also, every act has an associated value ai\mathbf{a_i}ai​ which gets added to the audience's anger if that act fails\text{fails}fails. If the anger becomes very large, people will start throwing sharp objects at Niket.

Since he is going to perform in the K\text{K}K-Pop World Festival, he is interested in knowing the minimum possible anger value of audience in the hypothetical situation where he was able to successfully execute exactly K\text{exactly K}exactly K acts during his performance. This means that the remaining acts either failed or could not happen (because of the dependency constraints).


Input Format
The first line contains a single interger T\text{T}T - the number of testcases.


For each testcase,



The first line contains two space separated integers N\text{N}N and K\text{K}K.
The following N-1 lines contain the information about the acts 2 to N. ith of these lines contains two space separated integers pip_ipi​ (pip_ipi​ < i+1) and aia_iai​- This means that the (i+1)th act is directly dependent upon the pip_ipi​th act and adds aia_iai​ to the anger value.


Output Format
For each testcase, print a line containing the minimum anger value of audience if Niket could perform exactly K\text{K}K of the acts successfully.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T - the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two space-separated integers N and K. The following N-1 lines contain the information about the acts 2 to N. The ith of these lines contains two space-separated integers pi and ai - This means that the (i+1)th act is directly dependent upon the pith act and adds ai to the anger value.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a line containing the minimum anger value of the audience if Niket could perform exactly K of the acts successfully.

sampleTestCases.explanation: If Niket fails on act 3, a3 = 4 gets added to the audience's anger and the act 5 can never happen. This means only 3 acts 1,2 and 4 are successful.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a dynamic programming approach with bit manipulation. We will create a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] represents the minimum anger value if we have performed i acts and j of them were successful. We will iterate over all possible subsets of acts and update dp[i][j] accordingly.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * 2^N) - because we are iterating over all possible subsets of acts for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * 2^N) - because we need to store the dp array for each test case.
